如何在家中减少吸入微塑料

How to breathe in fewer microplastics in your home

www.bbc.com 作者 vinni2 分数 72 评论 87 2026/04/11 21:01
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 即使灰尘沉降下来，当受到任何形式的摩擦干扰时，它也很容易被踢到空中。法国国家科学研究中心的生物地球化学家杰罗恩·桑克 (Jeroen Sonke) 最近与人合着了一项关于室内空气中微塑料的研究，调查了 1 到 10 微米范围内的小型微塑料的存在，这些微塑料比较大的碎片更能渗入肺部。桑克和他的同事在法国图卢兹的三套公寓内进行了取样，并从两辆车内收集了更多样本（发达国家的人们通常会在车内花费另外 5% 的时间）。时间）。松克惊讶地发现，室内一立方米空气中平均有超过500个碎片，而汽车驾驶舱内则有超过2200个碎片。他指出，这些样本主要来自停滞的空气，但如果有人在场扬起灰尘，空气中可能会有更多的微塑料。对于在地上爬行的婴儿来说，他们的脸距离充满微塑料的灰尘可能只有几英寸远。 Sonke 和他的同事计算出，婴儿平均每天可能吸入的微塑料量为 19,000 到 75,000 个 1-10 微米的颗粒。对于成年人来说，每天有 28,000 到 108,000 个颗粒。这些数字附带一个警告：科学家们要明确测量空气中微塑料的数量仍然具有挑战性。埃默里学院环境健康副教授道格拉斯·沃克表示：“没有测量微塑料的黄金标准方法，而且可能永远不会有。”他指出，这使得很难比较研究之间的结果。测量较小的微塑料尤其困难，因为它们可以更深入地进入肺部。 Sonke 指出，尽管技术正在不断改进，但标准显微镜的下限往往约为 20 微米。他的团队使用的方法（拉曼显微镜）首次使他们能够将微塑料范围降至 1 微米。 Getty Images 纺织工人比许多人接触到更高水平的微塑料污染，这可能会带来健康风险（图片来源：Getty Images）如果您拥有洗衣机，您可以为洗衣机购买一个过滤器，它可以减少废水中高达 90% 的微纤维。世界自然基金会塑料和材料科学团队的高级项目官员安妮莉丝·阿德里安（Annelise Adrian）也建议洗更多的衣服，以减少洗衣过程中衣服之间的摩擦。赖特补充说，除了技术创新之外，我们也可以改变我们的习惯。 “你可以尝试尽可能提高效率，只在需要时才洗衣服，”她说。她补充道，当天气和交通允许时，您可以考虑在室外晾干衣服。改穿纯天然材料制成的衣服，如棉、羊毛或亚麻，也可以减少与纤维微塑料的接触。与塑料不同，天然纤维在体内会分解。然而，它们往往比合成服装更贵，并且需要更多的土地和其他资源来制造。例如，生产棉纤维比合成纤维需要更多的水。 纺织工人 当日复一日地与纺织纤维打交道的人时，纺织纤维带来的健康风险成为人们关注的焦点。最近的一项研究分析了几家纺织工厂空气中的微塑料浓度，发现空气中和工厂车间的细颗粒（0.5 微米或更小）浓度始终很高。一些研究发现，这种大小的碎片会带来健康风险，包括哮喘、肺组织损伤和内分泌干扰等呼吸道问题。如果你要穿合成纤维衣服，购买二手衣服也可能会限制微塑料的暴露，尽管科学家们对此存在一定分歧。 “有些人说旧衣服已经脱落了松散的纤维，并且不易脱落，”阿德里安说。 “然而，其他人则认为，旧衣服在经过多次洗涤后会积聚更多的微塑料，而且随着时间的推移，纤维会断裂和变弱。”相反，购买旧的天然纤维衣服，如有机棉或有机羊毛产品，将是“黄金标准”，阿德里安说。赖特说，在尽量减少周围的合成纺织品后，下一步最好是尝试清除家里的塑料灰尘。 吸尘 彻底的吸尘可以吸走地板上的微塑料和家具，但它可以在您清洁时同时将颗粒物送回空气中。巴尔说，当你把袋子或罐子倒进垃圾箱时，你也会把脸塞满。“[吸尘]的作用是重新悬浮没有吸走的颗粒，”巴尔说。她建议在吸尘或除尘时佩戴 N95 口罩可能有助于保护您。赖特还建议在通风良好的空间中用吸尘器和清空机器中的灰尘。根据我们从其他类型的微型设备中了解到的情况她说，根据我们对石棉或碳纳米管等矿物纤维的了解，这些尺寸是有问题的。 “我们知道，这些纤维——小到足以像标枪一样刺入我们呼吸道末端的气囊——是有问题的。” 更多类似的：• 你的厨房里充满了微塑料。以下是如何少吃它们•微塑料如何影响我们的健康？•如果我们停止使用塑料会发生什么？赖特说，对于微塑料纤维，情况仍不清楚。 “它们会产生同样的效果吗？或者它们很柔韧，而矿物纤维非常坚硬，这是否会使[微塑料]毒性更小，危害更小？”除了它们可能干扰组织之外，人们还担心微塑料经常伴随而来。它们是多种细菌的家园，也可能携带其他污染物。有人认为，微塑料可能会放大环境中其他污染物的毒性。然而，这一领域的研究仍处于早期阶段，微塑料对身体的全面影响仍不清楚。要解决微塑料污染危机，需​​要的不仅仅是家庭内部的改变——还存在许多更广泛的可持续性问题。例如，如果您打算用天然纤维取代家中的合成纤维，还需要考虑使用有机棉会增加水和土地的使用量。或者，如果选择让家里多通风以赶走微塑料，那么污染只会被推到室外。如果缺乏系统性变革以及全球每年生产 4.6 亿吨塑料的减少，个人能做的就只有这么多。 “这很棘手，不是吗？”赖特承认了。 “因为我觉得在这些情况下，你需要选择战斗。”——要在收件箱中获取重要的气候新闻和充满希望的发展，请订阅《未来地球》时事通讯，而《基本清单》每周两次提供精选的专题和见解。如需了解 BBC 的更多科学、技术、环境和健康故事，请在 Facebook 和 Instagram 上关注我们。

Original Summary

  1. Even though dust settles, it can easily get kicked up into the air when disturbed by any sort of friction. Jeroen Sonke, a biogeochemist at the French National Center for Scientific Research who co-authored a recent study on indoor airborne microplastics, investigating the presence of small microplastics in the range of one to 10 micrometres, which penetrate deeper into the lungs than larger fragments.Sonke and his colleagues looked inside three of their own apartments in Toulouse, France, to take samples, and collected further samples from inside two cars (where people in developed countries typically spend another 5% of their time). Sonke was surprised to find an average of more than 500 fragments in one cubic metre of air indoors, and more than 2,200 fragments inside the cabin of a car. He noted that these samples were mainly from stagnant air, but if people were present stirring up dust, the air would likely have many more microplastics.For a baby crawling on the ground, their faces can be inches away from microplastic-filled dust. Sonke and his colleagues calculated the amount of microplastics an infant may inhale on an average day ranged between 19,000 and 75,000 particles in the 1-10 micrometre range. For adults, it was 28,000 to 108,000 particles a day.These numbers come with a caveat: it remains challenging for scientists to unequivocally measure the amount of microplastics in the air. "There's no gold standard approach for measuring microplastics, and there may never be," says Douglas Walker, an associate professor of environmental health at Emory College, noting this makes it difficult to compare findings between studies. And it's especially hard to measure smaller microplastics, which can find their way deeper into the lungs. Standard microscopes tend to have a lower limit of around 20 micrometres, though the technology is improving, Sonke notes. The method his team used (Raman microscopy) allowed them to get down to the 1 micrometre microplastic range for the first time.Getty ImagesTextile workers are exposed to higher levels of microplastic pollution than many, which may come with health risks (Credit: Getty Images)If you own your washing machine, you can purchase a filter for your washing machine that can reduce up to 90% of microfibres from wastewater. Annelise Adrian, a senior programme officer with the plastics and material science team at World Wildlife Fund, also recommends doing larger loads of laundry to reduce friction between clothes during the process.Wright adds that alongside technological innovations, we can change our habits too. "You can try and be as efficient as possible and only wash clothes when you need to," she says. When weather and access permit, you could consider drying your clothes outside, she adds.Switching to clothes made exclusively from natural materials, such as cotton, wool or linen, can also reduce your exposure to fibrous microplastics. Unlike plastic, natural fibres break down in the body. However, they tend to be pricier than synthetic clothing and require more land and other resources to make. Cotton fibres, for example, require substantially more water to produce than synthetic fibres.Textile workersThe health risks from textile fibres come into sharp focus when looking at those who work with them day in, day out. A recent study analysing airborne microplastic concentrations in several textile factories found consistently high concentrations of fine particles (0.5 micrometres and smaller) in the air and on factory floors. Some studies have found fragments of that size are responsible for health risks, including respiratory issues such as asthma, lung tissue damage and endocrine disruption.If you are going to wear synthetic clothes, buying used, second-hand garments may also limit microplastic exposure, although scientists are somewhat divided on this. "Some are saying that used clothes have already shed their loose fibres and are less prone to shedding," says Adrian. "However, others argue that used clothes can accumulate more microplastics from all the washes they've undergone, and fibres can break and weaken over time."Instead, buying used natural-fibre clothes, such as organic cotton or organic wool products, would be "the gold standard", says Adrian.After minimising the synthetic textiles around you, a good next step is to try to remove the plastic dust in your home, says Wright.Vacuuming A thorough vacuum can suck up microplastics that have settled on your floors and furniture, but it can simultaneously send particles back into the air as you clean. You'll also get a face full when you empty the bag or canister into the bin, says Barr."What [vacuuming] does do is it resuspends particles that it's not picking up," says Barr. She suggests wearing an N95 mask while vacuuming or dusting may help protect you. Wright also recommends vacuuming and emptying the dust from the machine in a well-ventilated space.Based on what we know from other types of tiny fibres, those dimensions are problematic, she says, based on what we know about mineral fibres such as asbestos or carbon nanotubes. "We know that those fibres – small enough to javelin down into the air sacs at the end of our airways – are problematic."More like this:• Your kitchen is full of microplastics. Here's how to eat less of them• How do microplastics affect our health?• What would happen if we stopped using plastic?For microplastic fibres, the picture is still unclear, says Wright. "Would they cause the same effect? Or the fact that they're flexible, whereas mineral fibres are very rigid, does that make [microplastics] less toxic, less harmful?"Beyond their potential to interfere with tissues, there's also the concern that microplastics often come with company. They are home to a broad range of bacteria, and may carry other contaminants too. It's been suggested that microplastics might act to amplify the toxicity of other pollutants in the environment. This field of research, however, is still in its early days, and the full effects of microplastics on the body are still not yet understood.To solve the microplastic pollution crisis will take a lot more than changes within the home – there are plenty of broader sustainability concerns too. If moving to replace synthetic fibres in your home with natural fibres, for instance, there's also the greater water and land use from organic cotton use to think about. Or if choosing to ventilate your home more to usher away microplastics, that pollution is only being pushed outdoors. Short of systemic change and a global reduction from the 460 million tonnes of plastic made each year, there's only so much individuals can do. "It's tricky, isn't it?" Wright concedes. "Because I feel like in these scenarios, it's pick your battles."--For essential climate news and hopeful developments to your inbox, sign up to the Future Earth newsletter, while The Essential List delivers a handpicked selection of features and insights twice a week.For more science, technology, environment and health stories from the BBC, follow us on Facebook and Instagram.

评论（默认中文）

dijit 2026/04/11 21:36
实际上，减少微塑料摄入量的最佳方法似乎是避免在塑料碗中进行微波炉加热，并避免使用塑料瓶盛装软饮料和水。 （虽然罐头内部实际上也使用了一层塑料薄膜……所以，也许只是避免包装水？）

超越你的 个人的 虽然有更大的鱼的摄入量。

汽车轮胎是微塑料进入河流的第一大来源，而且它甚至不是 关闭 （它们被认为是高达 85% 的环境微塑料的来源）。

这些颗粒物不会消失，它们最终会进入土壤和水道，最终进入你体内，无论你做什么。

Realistically, the best thing you can do to reduce your microplastics intake seems to be to avoid microwaving in plastic bowls and to avoid using plastic bottles for soft drinks and water. (Though cans actually use a thin film of plastic inside too.. so, maybe just avoid packaged water?)

Beyond your personal intake though there's bigger fish.

Car tyres are the #1 source for microplastics entering rivers, and it's not even close (they're thought to be the source of up to 85% of all environmental microplastics).

Those particulates don't just vanish, they end up in the soil and the waterways and it ends up inside you, no matter what you do.

在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 21:39 

  >microwaving in plastic bowls
更一般地说，切勿让热食物接触塑料。高温才是损坏塑料表面的原因，微波并没有什么特别之处。

例如，塑料茶袋在热水中也会发生同样的情况： https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S004565352...

  >microwaving in plastic bowls
More generally, never let hot food touch plastics. The high temperature is what damages the plastic surface, not anything special about microwaves.

For instance the same thing happens with plastic tea bags in hot water: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S004565352...

等等，什么？

有_塑料_茶袋吗？真的吗？

不知道我们已经达到了这种退化程度！ :-D

我想到了另一个例子：在许多欧洲国家，在超市的“奶酪角/酒吧”，每次切割一块奶酪时，他们都会去除箔纸，切割奶酪，然后用新的箔纸重新包装 - 这对于每个超市的每个奶酪吧：每年仅一个分支就浪费多少公里？

Wait, WHAT?

There are _plastic_ tea bags? Really?

Didnt know that we reached that level of degredation already! :-D

Another example comes to my mind: In lot of European conutries, at "cheese corner/bar" in the supermarket, every time a piece of cheese is cut, they are removing the foil, cutting the cheese, and then re-packing it in new foil after that - and this for every chees bar in every supermarket: How much kilometers does just one branch waste per year?

The kicker? It's only on high-end tea, because it's more expensive than regular tea bags.

KellyCriterion 2026/04/11 22:33

Curious: What is "high end tea"? Or is this just another wording for "premium-markup" which makes a product more expensive?
在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 22:40
是的，我只是指货架上更贵的茶。对于更便宜的 SKU，他们试图降低成本，因此他们使用普通的茶包。塑料袋已经成为一种趋势，但希望大多数品牌都已经放弃了。

这项研究很有趣，因为他们随后使用 SEM 对塑料进行成像，您可以看到塑料表面的实际情况 撕碎 只需接触热水即可在微观层面上实现。

塑料具有低能量表面，这意味着不需要太多能量就能将其撕裂。即使布朗运动也足够了，这非常疯狂。

Yes I just mean the more expensive tea on the shelf. On cheaper SKUs they're trying to cut cost so they use normal tea bags. The plastic sachets were a trend for a couple years but hopefully most brands have switched away.

That study is interesting because they used SEM to image the plastic afterward, and you can see how the plastic surface has literally been torn up on a microscopic level simply by touching hot water.

Plastic has a low-energy surface, which means it doesn't take much energy to tear it apart. Even Brownian motion is enough, which is pretty wild.

Is there any real difference between the more expensive shelf places ("on eye height") than the more cheap one?

在 HN 中查看
tpm 2026/04/12 03:33
> 或者昂贵的产品的质量/味道真的有区别吗？

如果我们仍然谈论茶，那么当然存在巨大的差异。而且最好的茶不会包装在单独的茶袋中（也不会在超市出售，除非是一个茶文化非常高的国家）。

因此，在低端市场，你会看到用大量化学物质种植的茶叶，由机器或工资微薄的工人采摘，经过大量工业加工，在国际市场上批量出售。而在最高端，你会得到不涉及化学物质的手工小批量茶，可能以某种特殊的方式种植，比如遮荫的茶树或森林地区百年老茶树，手工加工，这样叶子就不会破碎等等……所有这些都反映在味道上。

> Or is there really a difference in the quality/taste of the expensive ones?

If we are still talking about tea, then of course there are huge differences. And the best tea is not packaged in individual tea bags (also it's not sold in supermarkets unless it's a country with a very high tea culture).

So at the low end you would have tea that is grown with lots of chemicals, plucked by machines or by badly paid workers, industrially processed in high quantities, sold as bulk on international markets. While on the highest end you would have artisanal small-batch tea with no chemicals involved, possibly grown in some special way like the tea bushes shaded from the sun or hundred years old tea trees in forested areas, processed by hand so the leaves are not broken etc... And all of this is reflected in the taste.

在 HN 中查看
lancewiggs 2026/04/12 06:41
还要补充的是，茶叶在产地进行分级，买家根据等级进行购买。因此，低质量的茶袋中的茶客观上会比高质量的茶差，而最好的茶却永远不会靠近袋。
And to add - tea is graded at source, and buyers purchase based on grade. So a low quality tea bag will have tea that is objectively worse than a high quality one, while the best tea is never near a bag.

all the high end tea bags I've seen are silk

OutOfHere 2026/04/11 21:53
有些人使用塑料切割托盘/刀子/叉子/勺子/杯子/水壶，这些也是要避免的。

我还会避免使用所有不粘锅和餐具，因为它们内衬有比塑料更糟糕的 PFAS，而且它会慢慢地分解到食物中。谨防本论坛上的行业骗子，因为他们会让您忽略这样一个事实：众所周知，摄入 PFAS 会导致血液中 PFAS 浓度升高。

Some people use plastic cutting-trays / knives / forks /spoons / cups / jugs, which also are some things to avoid.

I would also avoid all nonstick pans and utensils, as they're lined with PFAS which is worse than plastic, and slowly it will break off into the food. Beware the industry shills on this forum, as they will have you ignore the fact that ingesting PFAS is well known to result in higher blood levels of PFAS.

array_key_first 2026/04/12 06:41
不粘锅使用起来非常安全。但是，如果您担心，请避免非常高的温度并使用木制器皿，而不是金属器皿。
Non stick pans are perfectly safe to use. But, if you're worried, just avoid very high temperatures and use wooden utensils on them, never metal.

alt227 2026/04/11 22:04
完全同意你的观点，但是从平底锅里吃少量的 PFAS 似乎是无毒的。

https://youtu.be/SC2eSujzrUY

Fully agree with you, however eating small bits of PFAS from pans seems to be pretty non toxic.

Even in the recent Veritasium video about it they said that unless the chemical was heated to above ~300 degress C if will pass through the human digestive system without causing any harm.

Curious why you say this when the linked article says the best thing you can do is avoid synthetic textiles
在 HN 中查看
dijit 2026/04/11 21:40
文章列出了几件事，包括纺织品、塑料包装和避免轮胎颗粒。我以容器/瓶子为主导，因为那似乎是单次暴露最集中的地方（在塑料、瓶装水中微波），但你说得对，纺织品也在那里，尤其是空气中的微纤维。

食品包装的暴露程度比聚酯要显着许多倍，随着时间的推移，聚酯会减缓水滴的速度。

the article lists several things including textiles, plastic packaging, and avoiding tyre particles. I led with containers/bottles because that's where the most concentrated single exposures seem to be (microwaving in plastic, bottled water), but you're right that textiles are up there too, especially for airborne microfibres.

The exposure from food packaging is many times more prominent than polyester, which slows down leeching over time.

在 HN 中查看
sebmellen 2026/04/11 21:42
纳米塑料/化学浸出（加热食品容器时会发生这种情况）与从衣服和其他塑料材料上脱落的微塑料（这就是本文所讨论的内容）之间存在很大差异。不过，两者都是重要问题。
There’s a big difference between nanoplastics/chemical leeching (which is what happens with heated food containers) and microplastics of the sort that break off from clothing other plastic materials (which is what the article is talking about). Both are significant issues though.

另外，停止使用包裹有可溶解塑料层的洗碗盒和洗衣盒。请使用粉末或液体清洁剂代替。如果您在商店中找不到，请在网上查找，因为它肯定有库存。

One action doesn't obviate the need for another.

Also, stop using dishwashing pods and laundry pods with the dissolvable plastic layer encasing them. Use powder or liquid detergent instead. If you can't find it in store, look for it online, because it definitely is in stock.

array_key_first 2026/04/12 06:43

在 HN 中查看
scotty79 2026/04/11 22:11
汽车涉及更高的能量，类似的东西往往会随着能量的平方或立方而增长。如果汽车每英里造成的塑料污染比自行车多几个数量级，我不会感到惊讶。
With cars there are higher energies involved, and things like that tend to grow with square or cube of the energy. I wouldn't be surprised if car was causing orders of magnitude more plastic pollution than a bike per person per mile.
在 HN 中查看
Gigachad 2026/04/11 22:16
思考起来非常直观。轮胎的整个胎面都变成了磨碎的塑料粉尘。汽车一年消耗的轮胎比自行车多得多。
It’s quite intuitive to think about. The entire tread of the tire becomes ground up plastic dust. Cars burn through significantly more KGs of tires in a year than a bicycle.
在 HN 中查看
alt227 2026/04/11 22:14

在 HN 中查看
bertil 2026/04/11 22:11
轮胎的损坏量与每轴重量的四次方成正比。这意味着，对于相同的行程，自行车棚 (3000 公斤/80 公斤)^4 减少了约 2000 万倍。假设橡胶是相同的，而不是它具有可能含有或不含有致癌物质的专有化学物质。八个数量级的差异感觉是相关的。

我知道的几乎所有火车都使用金属轮。我们可以看看少数没有这样做的人，但有件事告诉我，提出这一论点的人不想考虑替代的车轮结构，而是希望播下怀疑的种子，私下里，大厅用数千辆汽车取代一辆地铁，我不确定这是一个好主意。

所以，请不要带着这些废话进来。

The amount of damage to tires is proportional to the fourth power of the weight per axel. That means that for the same journey, a bike sheds (3000 kg / 80 kg)ˆ4 so about 20 million times less. Assuming that the rubber is the same—and not that it has proprietary chemistry that may or may not contain carcinogens. Eight orders of magnitude of difference feels relevant.

Almost every train I know use metal wheels. We can look at the few that don’t, but something tells me people who raise that argument don’t want to look at alternative wheel composition, but rather hope to seed doubt and, in private, lobby replace one metro with thousands of cars, and I’m not sure that’s a good idea.

So, please, don’t come in here with that bullshit.

在 HN 中查看
alt227 2026/04/11 22:17
可爱的语气，听起来你是一个非常好的人。

我根本没有提到火车轮子。他们的刹车片由碳、陶瓷和树脂化合物制成。它们像任何刹车片一样磨损，因此造成同样的灰尘污染。请记住，世界上有些火车长度超过一英里，有超过 1000 个车轮。

我没有看到有人声称自行车或火车造成的影响与汽车接近相同的程度，但重要的是要记住它们仍然会造成一些影响，因此它们并不是灵丹妙药。为了彻底消除人类交通系统中的这些污染物，解决方案仍然需要改进。

Lovely tone, you sound like a really nice person.

I never mentioned train wheels at all. Their brake pads are made from things like carbon, ceramic, and resin compounds. These wear down like any brake pads and so cause the same dust pollution. Remember that some trains in the world are over a mile long and have over 1000 wheels.

I dont see anybody claiming that bikes or trains cause anywhere near the same level as cars, but it is important to remember that they still do cause some and so they are not a silver bullet. Solutions still need advancing in order to completely remove these pollutants from human transportation systems.

在 HN 中查看
dijit 2026/04/11 22:31
“自行车也会导致洪水”在技术上是正确的，就像水龙头滴水和水坝决堤都会导致洪水一样。这个框架的效果（有意或无意）表明，在解决导致 0.01% 问题的问题之前，我们不应该优先考虑导致 99.99% 问题的问题。这不是一个严肃的立场，你只是在保护你的舒适。
'Bikes cause it too' is technically true in the way that a dripping tap and a burst dam both cause flooding. The effect of this framing (intentional or not) is to suggest we shouldn't prioritise the thing that causes 99.99% of the problem until we've solved the thing that causes 0.01%. That's not a serious position, you're just protecting your comfort.

My argument is 'Lets try to make all vehicle tyres and brakes from something that doesnt pollute the earth and harm animal life on it.'

在 HN 中查看
shubhamjain 2026/04/11 21:47
可能永远不会。我认为自从对它们的恐惧成为主流以来至少已有十年了。是的，这些东西可能需要一段时间才能显现出来，但考虑到它们存在的规模以及我们使用它们的时间，我们至少会看到它们与一些严重的健康问题之间存在某种明确的关系。看看这篇文章本身，对健康的影响很方便地隐藏在最后一节中，它只是一遍又一遍地重复它们如何在身体各处发现，但没有提及可能发生的情况。

很多恐慌都围绕着同样的框架，在母乳/血液中发现了“微塑料”，但从未有人提及它可能造成的后果。答案是“没什么”，是不是太难理解了？

Probably never. I think it's been at least a decade since the fear over them became mainstream. Yeah, it's possible these things can take time to show up but considering the scale of their presence and how long we have been using them, we would have at least seen some definite relationship between them and some serious health concern. Look at the article itself, the health impact is conveniently buried in the last section, and it just repeats over and over how they can found everywhere in the body but nothing on what can possible happen.

So much of the scare revolves around the same framing, "microplastic" have been found in breast milk/blood whatever, but never seen one mentioning what it can possibly cause. Is it too hard to fathom that the answer is "nothing"?

bertil 2026/04/11 22:14

Not as long as there are powerful car lobbies and the main source of microplastic will remain car tires.

在 HN 中查看
sigmoid10 2026/04/11 21:36
鉴于它们如此普遍，而且直到今天为止，在任何严格的研究中都没有发现微塑料与任何健康问题之间存在因果关系[1]，我想说，这些报道中的很多内容都是生态/有机行业散布恐惧的，针对的是对科学知之甚少的容易上当受骗的人。不像电子烟雾那样疯狂和不物理，但绝对远不及石棉。链接的文章甚至详细介绍了普通民众的观念有多么扭曲，以及医生应该如何更好地教育人们，因为有 真实的 来自其他事物的风险。如果您真的担心常见污染物对健康的影响，那么日常化合物中经实际证明的因果影响存在更大的风险。

[1] https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12620896/

Given the fact that they are so ubiquitous and yet no causal relation between microplastics and any health issue whatsoever has been identified in any rigorous study until today [1], I'd say a lot of this reporting is fear mongering by the eco/organic industry, aimed at gullible people who know very little about science. Not as insane and unphysical as electro smog, but definitely nowhere near asbestos. The linked article even goes into detail how warped the perceptions are among the general population and how doctors should educate people better, because there are real risks from other things out there. If you're really concerned about health effects of common pollutants, there are much bigger risks with actual proven causal effects in everyday compounds.

[1] https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12620896/

在 HN 中查看
strogonoff 2026/04/11 21:39
> BPA 是一种已知的内分泌干扰物。尽管最初被认为是一种弱环境雌激素，但最近的研究表明，BPA 在刺激某些细胞反应方面的效力可能与雌二醇相似。

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21605673/

> 2017 年，欧洲化学品管理局得出结论，由于 BPA 具有内分泌干扰物的特性，因此应将其列为高度关注的物质。 [30] 2023年，欧洲食品安全局重新评估了BFA的安全性，将每日耐受摄入量（TDI）大幅降低至0.2纳克（十亿分之二克），比2015年之前的TDI低了2万倍。

> 2012 年，美国食品和药物管理局 (FDA) 禁止在供 12 个月以下儿童使用的婴儿奶瓶中使用 BPA。[31]自然资源保护委员会称此举不够充分，并表示 FDA 需要禁止所有食品包装中使用双酚 A。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Health_effects_of_Bisphenol_A

> 在此之前，2024 年初发表了另一篇论文，其中一组意大利研究人员在早期心血管疾病患者的颈动脉（一对向大脑输送血液的主要血管）中发现的斑块中发现了微塑料。这将它们的存在与疾病进展恶化联系起来。在接下来的三年里，斑块中携带这些微塑料的人中风、心脏病或猝死的风险增加了 4.5 倍。

> 然后在 2025 年 2 月，另一组科学家在人类尸体的大脑中发现了微塑料。最值得注意的是，那些生前被诊断出患有痴呆症的人，其大脑中的塑料含量是未患痴呆症的人的 10 倍。 “我们很震惊，”领导这项研究的新墨西哥大学毒理学教授马修·坎彭说。

https://www.bbc.co.uk/future/article/20250723-how-do-the-mic...

> BPA is a known endocrine disruptor. Although initially considered to be a weak environmental estrogen, more recent studies have demonstrated that BPA may be similar in potency to estradiol in stimulating some cellular responses.

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21605673/

> In 2017 the European Chemicals Agency concluded that BPA should be listed as a substance of very high concern due to its properties as an endocrine disruptor.[30] In 2023, the European Food Safety Authority re-evaluated the safety of BFA and significantly reduced tolerable daily intake (TDI) to 0.2 nanograms (0.2 billionths of a gram), 20,000 times lower than the previous TDI from 2015.

> In 2012, the United States' Food and Drug Administration (FDA) banned the use of BPA in baby bottles intended for children under 12 months.[31] The Natural Resources Defense Council called the move inadequate, saying the FDA needed to ban BPA from all food packaging.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Health_effects_of_Bisphenol_A

> This followed another paper in early 2024, where a group of Italian researchers identified microplastics in plaques found in the carotid arteries – a pair of major vessels which deliver blood to the brain – of people with early-stage cardiovascular disease. This linked their presence to worsening disease progression. Over the following three years, individuals carrying these microplastics in their plaques had a 4.5-fold greater risk of stroke, heart attack or sudden death.

> Then in February 2025, another group of scientists identified microplastics in the brains of human cadavers. Most notably, those who had been diagnosed with dementia prior to their death had up to 10 times as much plastic in their brains compared to those without the condition. "We were shocked," says Matthew Campen, a University of New Mexico toxicology professor who led this study.

https://www.bbc.co.uk/future/article/20250723-how-do-the-mic...

sigmoid10 2026/04/11 21:43
这正是我所说的那种散布恐惧的报道，并解释了我上面链接的研究评论中描述的公众扭曲的看法。如果你观察患有痴呆症的死者的大脑，你还会发现更多的铝，这导致人们对止汗剂感到恐慌。但如果你的科学方法正确的话，表明铝接触会导致痴呆症的实际因果证据为零。顺便说一句也是如此。淀粉样斑块，这实际上阻碍了真正的阿尔茨海默病研究。因此，即使是科学家也无法避免相关性！=因果关系问题。您可以通过比较相似的分子并发明生物利用度途径来弥补各种潜在危险。但归根结底，这只是猜测，您需要确凿的数据来证明这些假设。
This is exactly the kind of fear mongering reporting I was talking about and explains the general public's warped perception described in the research review I linked above. If you look at the brains of dead people with dementia, you'll also find more aluminum, which has caused people to panic about antiperspirants. But there is zero actual causal evidence that Al exposure causes dementia, if you do the science right. The same goes btw. for amyloid plaques, which has actually hindered real Alzheimer's research. So not even scientists are safe from the correlation!=causality problem. You can make up all kinds of potential hazards by comparing similar molecules and inventing bioavailability pathways. But at the end of the day this is just speculation and you need hard data to prove these assumptions.

OutOfHere 2026/04/11 21:56
铝的关系很容易通过以下观察来解释：健康的肾脏可以很好地排泄铝，而不健康的肾脏则不能，因此铝会积累。根据大脑固有的清除化学物质的能力，大脑中的铝沉积也可能存在类似的变化。相比之下，塑料的排泄机制似乎不太值得信赖。

用户故意且公然地忽略了现有的大量科学文献。此外，塑料还与许多其他有害化学物质捆绑在一起，例如邻苯二甲酸盐、双酚等。风险不仅存在于大脑中，还存在于血管中，包括与心脏相邻的血管。

请注意本页上的塑料行业托词。他们会让你忽视科学，变得不育，然后让你死去，这一切都是为了他们的暂时利益。

The aluminum relations are easily explained with the observation that healthy kidneys excrete aluminum well, whereas unhealthy kidneys don't and so it accumulates. There might also be similar variations in aluminum deposition in the brain depending on the brain's innate ability to wash out chemicals. In contrast, the excretory mechanisms of plastics seems less trustworthy.

The user is deliberately and blatantly ignoring a wealth of scientific literature that exists. Also, plastics come bundled with numerous other harmful classes of chemicals, e.g. phthalates, bisphenols, etc. The risk is not merely in the brain, but also in blood vessels, including those adjacent to the heart.

Beware the plastics industry shills on this page. They will have you ignore the science, become infertile, and then have you die, all for their temporary gain.

dijit 2026/04/11 22:20
当然。这里有一些：

- 76% 的人类精液样本中发现了微塑料，接触 PET 的男性精子活力降低： https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12299061/

- 中国多地点研究（113 名男性），PTFE 微塑料与精子功能障碍有关（发表在 eBioMedicine/Lancet 上）： https://www.thelancet.com/journals/ebiom/article/PIIS2352-39...

- 每个人类睾丸样本中都发现了微塑料，其浓度是狗的 3 倍，其中 PVC 与犬科动物精子数量减少相关： https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36948312/

- 人类精液在体外暴露于聚苯乙烯 MP 中，表现出随时间变化的活力下降和 DNA 碎片增加： https://www.mdpi.com/2305-6304/13/7/605

我之前链接的小鼠研究并不是全部。这是一件。到 2022 年，“没有人类证据”的说法或许还能站得住脚，但现在已经不再是这样了。

另外，回复：“1000 mg/L 是不现实的”..该研究使用了两种剂量，100 μg/L 和 1000 μg/L。阿姆斯特丹的原地表水经测量约为 50 微克/升。较低的实验剂量完全在现实世界污染的一个数量级之内。这就是剂量反应科学的工作原理。

将此与顺势疗法进行比较是……一种选择。

Sure. Here's a few:

- Microplastics found in 76% of human semen samples, with PET-exposed men showing reduced sperm motility: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12299061/

- Multi-site study across China (113 men), PTFE microplastics linked to sperm dysfunction (published in eBioMedicine/Lancet): https://www.thelancet.com/journals/ebiom/article/PIIS2352-39...

- Microplastics found in every human testicle sampled, at 3x the concentration of dogs, with PVC correlating to lower sperm count in canines: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36948312/

- In-vitro exposure of human semen to polystyrene MPs showed time-dependent decline in motility and increased DNA fragmentation: https://www.mdpi.com/2305-6304/13/7/605

The mouse study I linked earlier isn't the whole picture; it's one piece. The "no human evidence" line was maybe defensible in 2022. It isn't anymore.

Also, re: "1000 mg/L is unrealistic".. the study used two doses, 100 μg/L and 1000 μg/L. Raw surface water in Amsterdam has been measured at ~50 μg/L. The lower experimental dose is well within an order of magnitude of real-world contamination. That's how dose-response science works.

Comparing this to homeopathy is… a choice.

sigmoid10 2026/04/11 22:29
如果我只解释第一个，请原谅我，因为其他的似乎都是多余的（如果你看看作者的话，并不是说可疑的多余）。这些都不是我所要求的元评论，但这次我会放过它。

第一的：

>未发现 MP 暴露与精子浓度或精子总数之间存在显着关联

第二：N=34

第三（如果第二个没有泄露的话）：他们确实发现的一个效应是 p=0.056。这意味着十八分之一的随机研究会仅仅因为概率统计而发现这种效应。正如您很好地指出的那样，有很多这样的研究。如果您用自己的心态进行研究，您就不会找到所有无效结果。但这正是寻求真理的科学家与试图在互联网上争论的业余爱好者的区别。

You'll excuse me if I only explain the first one, since the others seem redundant (not to say suspiciously redundant if you look at the authors). And none of this is a meta review like I asked, but I'll let it slide this time.

First:

>no significant association was found between MP exposure and sperm concentration or total sperm count

Second: N=34

Third (if second didn't give it away): The one effect they did find sits at p=0.056. That means one in 18 random studies will find that effect just because of probability statistics. And as you have nicely pointed out, there are maaaany studies like this out there. You just don't find all the null results if you go into research with your mindset. But that is exactly what differentiates a scientist looking for truth from a hobbyist trying to argue on the internet.

dijit 2026/04/11 22:34
您要求进行荟萃分析。以下是其中一项： 39 项研究，发表在《危险材料杂志》上：

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S03043...

研究发现，微塑料导致精子浓度下降599万/mL，精子活力下降14.62%，精子活力下降23.56%，精子异常率增加10.65%。 （我直接从源复制并粘贴这些值）。

你说过，如果有人向你展示严格的因果关系，你会“立即加入”。这是一项荟萃分析，其中绘制了从分子起始事件 (ROS) 到组织水平损伤的因果链的不良结果路径。这与人体临床试验之前的严格程度差不多，（出于明显的道德原因）没有人会进行临床试验。

至于 p=0.056 的批评：您从四个链接之一中选择了最弱的单个数据点并宣布胜利（科学！）。在体外研究中，我在受控条件下将暴露的人类精液与微塑料联系起来，并观察到随着时间的推移，活力下降和 DNA 碎片增加。这不是简单的相关性，而是对人体组织的直接因果实验。你没有解决这个问题。

目标已经从“向我展示证据”转变为“向我展示元审查”，再到“好吧，不是那个元审查”。在某些时候，你必须关注研究的实际内容，而不是你想要的内容 喜欢 它说。

You asked for a meta-analysis. Here's one: 39 studies, published in the Journal of Hazardous Materials:

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S03043...

It found microplastics caused a decrease of 5.99 million/mL in sperm concentration, 14.62% in sperm motility, 23.56% in sperm viability, and a 10.65% increase in sperm abnormality rate. (I copied and pasted these values directly from the source).

You said you'd be "onboard immediately" if someone showed you a rigorous causal link. This is a meta-analysis with an adverse outcome pathway mapping the causal chain from molecular initiating event (ROS) through to tissue-level damage. That's about as rigorous as it gets before human clinical trials, which (for obvious ethical reasons) nobody is going to run.

As for the p=0.056 critique: you picked the weakest single data point from one of four links and declared victory (scientific!). The in-vitro study I linked exposed actual human semen to microplastics under controlled conditions and observed time-dependent decline in motility and increased DNA fragmentation. That's not a simple correlation, it's a direct causal experiment on human tissue. You didn't address it.

The goalposts have moved from "show me evidence" to "show me a meta-review" to "well not THAT meta-review." At some point you have to engage with what the research actually says rather than with what you'd like it to say.

dijit 2026/04/12 02:05
一项 N=34 的研究发现对精子数量没有显着影响，这并不与对 39 项研究的荟萃分析相矛盾。这就是荟萃分析的目的：将动力不足的个体研究汇总成具有统计意义的东西。

如果您拥有所声称的科学学位，您就知道这一点。

至于谷歌人工智能概述：如果这是你现在的证据标准，那么我们距离“我支持科学”已经走了很长一段路。

A single study with N=34 finding no significant effect on sperm count doesn't contradict a meta-analysis of 39 studies that did. That's what meta-analyses are for: aggregating underpowered individual studies into something statistically meaningful.

You know this if you have the science degree you're claiming.

As for Google AI Overview: if that's your standard of evidence now, we've come a long way from 'I subscribe to science.'

在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 21:30
https://archive.ph/UcCq6

说 HEPA 过滤器可以去除“99%”的微塑料是相当具有误导性的。

空气中颗粒物的大部分质量是较大尺寸的可见灰尘。然而，这些颗粒在到达空气净化器之前就会“脱落”。

最好的建议不是“仅使用 HEPA”或（本文中的一个奇怪的建议）“使用多级过滤器”，而是拥有一台空气净化器，其中 清洁空气输送率 (CADR) 与房间大小相匹配。为了过滤大灰尘，您需要大量气流，目标是每小时换气 6-8 次 (ACH)。

此外，盒子上的 CADR 始终处于最高风扇速度，对于在有人的房间中持续使用来说总是太大声。因此，理想情况下，您需要假设风扇速度产生 45 分贝或更低，来确定空气净化器的尺寸。 HouseFresh 是一个发布这些数字的优秀评论网站。

大多数人都大大缩小了空气净化器的尺寸，或者在非常低的风扇设置下运行它们，然后他们举手说空气净化器不起作用。

https://archive.ph/UcCq6

Saying HEPA filters remove "99%" of microplastic is pretty misleading.

Most of the mass in airborne particles is in the larger sizes of visible dust. However these particles will "fall out" before they reach the air purifier.

The best advice isn't "use only HEPA" or (an odd one, from this article) "use filters with multiple stages," it's to have an air purifier where the Clean Air Delivery Rate (CADR) is matched to room size. For filtering large dust you need a lot of air flow, aim for 6-8 Air Changes per Hour (ACH).

Also the CADR on the box is always on the highest fan speed, which is always way too loud for constant use in an occupied room. So ideally you want to size the air purifiers assuming a fan speed generating 45 decibels or less. HouseFresh is an excellent review site that publishes these numbers.

Most people dramatically undersize their air purifiers, or run them on a very low fan setting, and then they throw up their hands and say that air purifiers don't work.

在 HN 中查看
himata4113 2026/04/11 21:36
您也不是真的想使用 HEPA，您想最大化气流。

配备低 MERV 型过滤器的 PC 风扇效果非常好，因为颗粒越小（我认为这种效果在 5 微米以下才会出现），它的过滤效果就越好，因此，如果它能通过比高效空气过滤器多 10 倍的空气，那么它与高效空气过滤器一样有效，同时能够更快地过滤更多空气并保持颗粒在空气中。

唯一的缺点是颗粒范围较小，较低的 Merv 过滤器不足以过滤，因此 70% 以上的颗粒会通过

You don't really want to use HEPA either, you want to maximize airflow.

PC fans with low MERV type filter do great since the smaller the particle (I think this effect kicks in below 5 microns) the better it is at filtering it so if it can pass 10 times more air than a hepa filter it's as effective as one while being able to filter more air faster and keeping the particles airbone.

The only downside is that small range of particles where lower merv filters aren't good enough to filter so upwards of 70% of the particles pass through

在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 22:13
同意。 MERV 11-14 可以 远的 比 HEPA 更有效。

如果您需要“一次完成”过滤（例如将空气泵入医院手术室），那么您就需要 HEPA。大多数家用空气净化器将清洁空气混合回同一房间，因此 MERV 更接近理想的最佳点。

买也很重要 有信誉的 MERV 过滤器品牌，最好是具有大量折叠（表面积）的过滤器，如 3M 1900 MPR。在最近的测试中，大约一半的过滤器品牌的得分远低于其声称的 MERV 评级：

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FKAVek1YaSQ

Agreed. MERV 11-14 can be far more effective than HEPA.

If you need to filter "one and done" (like pumping air into a hospital operating room), that's where you need HEPA. Most home air purifiers mix the clean air back into the same room, so MERV is closer to the ideal sweet spot.

It's also important to buy reputable brands of MERV filter, ideally ones which have a large number of folds (surface area) like the 3M 1900 MPR. In recent testing about half of filter brands scored well below their claimed MERV rating:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FKAVek1YaSQ

That sounds worth knowing; however when I looked MERV up, it seems that it's a rating system, not a type of filter. Could you be more specific abot the kind of filter you mean?
在 HN 中查看
himata4113 2026/04/11 22:57
HEPA 通常只是 True HEPA 作为后代的一种过滤器，MERV 是一个系列，可让您在最高气流下准确过滤所需的东西。这实际上取决于您家中的污染类型。

如果您有大量灰尘，那么您需要尽可能高的气流（大约 MERV 9-10），如果您想过滤导致过敏的物质，则需要高达 MERV 14，因为 MERV 9-10 的有效性在该特定范围内非常低。

HEPA is typically just one type of filter with True HEPA as an offspring, MERV is a range which allows you to filter exactly what you need at the highest airflow. It really depends on what kind of pollution you have at your home.

If you just have a lot of dust then you want highest airflow possible (around MERV 9-10) if you want to filter things that cause allergies you need to go as high as MERV 14 since MERV 9-10 effectiveness is super low in that specific range.

在 HN 中查看
mcny 2026/04/11 21:39
> 大多数人都大大减小了空气净化器的尺寸，或者在非常低的风扇设置下运行它们，然后他们举手说空气净化器不起作用。

我相信这里有总比没有好。对空气过滤器最大的抱怨之一是噪音，所以也许一个好的折衷方案是在一段时间内全速和全噪音运行它们，或者在房间里没有人的情况下运行它们？

> Most people dramatically under-size their air purifiers, or run them on a very low fan setting, and then they throw up their hands and just say that air purifiers don't work.

I believe something is better than nothing here. One of the biggest complaints against air filters is noise so maybe a good compromise would be to run them at full speed and full noise for a certain amount of time or something when nobody is in the room?

在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 21:43
错误的安全感可能是 更糟 总比没有好，因为它会阻止你寻找真正有效的选择。

我也想要这样一个“害羞”的空气净化器，但制造商似乎总是走另一条路：当检测到有人占用时，他们 增加 风扇速度。

IMO 的最佳选择就是购买具有良好 CADR 分贝比的空气净化器，然后（再次）正确调整其尺寸。一个令人惊讶的好选择是 Airfanta 3 Pro，它基本上就像那些野火过滤盒，只是它使用 PC 风扇。

A false sense of security can be worse than nothing, because it prevents you from seeking out actually effective options.

I too would like such a "shy" air purifier, but manufacturers always seem to go the other way: when occupancy is detected they increase the fan speed.

Best option IMO is just to get an air purifier with a good CADR-to-decibel ratio and then (again) size it correctly. A surprisingly good option is something called the Airfanta 3 Pro, which is basically like those wildfire filter boxes except it uses PC fans.

hansonkd 2026/04/11 21:48
更大的过滤器=更少的噪音来移动更多的空气
bigger filter = less noise to move more air
在 HN 中查看
OutOfHere 2026/04/11 21:43
让空气净化器真正发挥作用的方法是将它们安装在进气口处，即窗户中，或中央进气口所在的位置，这样所有进入的空气都会通过它们。我也使用室内空气净化器，但不能代替进气口的空气净化器。请注意，轮胎和柴油烟雾是附近有害颗粒物的主要来源。

在不需要主动加热​​或冷却的春季和秋季，运行进气风扇并不昂贵。

The way to get air purifiers to really work well is to install them at the air intake, i.e. in the windows, or where the central air intake is, so all incoming air passes through them. I use indoor air purifiers too, but not as a substitute for ones at the intake. Note that tires and diesel fumes are prominent neighborhood sources of harmful particulates.

It is not expensive to run intake fans in the spring and fall seasons when active heating or cooling are not required.

在 HN 中查看
schiffern 2026/04/11 21:46
这有助于消除来自外部的污染（交通、花粉、木烟），但大多数微塑料是通过在家里移动/穿着合成纺织品而产生的。

正压系统很棒，我喜欢它们，但是有一个 定量的 在这种情况下不匹配。高于 ~1 ACH 时，您的 HVAC 成本将会飞涨（即使有热/湿度回收），但为了有效过滤，您需要 6-8 ACH 才能在肺部之前捕获较大的灰尘。

That helps for pollution that comes from outside (traffic, pollen, wood smoke), but most of the microplastics are generated by moving/wearing synthetic textiles inside the home.

Positive pressure systems are great, love 'em, but there's a quantitative mismatch in this case. Above ~1 ACH your HVAC costs will go through the roof (even with heat/humidity recovery), but for effective filtration you need 6-8 ACH to catch the larger dust before your lungs do.

Gigachad 2026/04/11 22:18
而对于 pm2.5，至少在澳大利亚，它完全是由烹饪产生的。外面的空气非常干净。
And for pm2.5, at least in Australia, it’s entirely generated from cooking. Outside air is very clean.
在 HN 中查看
smallerize 2026/04/11 21:36
“微塑料”一词包括最大 5 毫米的颗粒。我认为大部分材料可能是由这些较大的颗粒组成的。但我猜你不太可能吸入那么大的东西。因此，虽然空气过滤器可以去除您可能吸入的大部分塑料，但您仍然需要清理家里的大部分微塑料。
The therm "microplactics" includes particles up to 5mm. And I think the bulk of the material is probably made of these larger particles. But I guess you're less likely to inhale something that large. So while air filters will remove most of the plastic you might inhale, you will still have to clean up most of the mass of microplastics in your house.
在 HN 中查看
perrygeo 2026/04/12 01:05
塑料健康灾难让我感到惊讶的是，任何理性的决策过程都会认为这些化合物是全新的，没有进化相关性。应适用预防原则；公司应该有责任证明这些化合物的安全性。相反，它们被错误地认为是安全的、大规模部署的，现在我们有责任应对其影响。

这是一次未经同意、没有责任的实验。就像我们对大气二氧化碳的实验一样，这些公司通过将负债转嫁给公众来获利。

What amazes me about the plastic health debacle is that any rational decision making process would have identified these compounds as entirely novel, with no evolutionary correlate. The precautionary principle should have applied; the burden should have been on corporations to prove these compounds safe. Instead, they were incorrectly assumed to be safe, deployed at scale, and now the burden is on us to deal with the impacts.

This was an experiment run without consent, and without accountability. Much like our experiment with atmospheric CO2, these companies are making profits by dumping the liabilities onto the public.

xnx 2026/04/12 03:02
我完全支持改善室内空气质量 (IAQ)，但与其他污染物（挥发性有机化合物、二氧化碳等）相比，微塑料只是一个非常小的问题。对于大多数人来说，过滤室外空气交换是最好的解决办法。
I'm all for improving indoor air quality (IAQ), but microplastics are a very minor concern compared to other pollutants (VOCs, CO2, etc.). Filtered outdoor air exchange is the best fix for most of them.

himata4113 2026/04/11 21:54
如果有人正在考虑购买空气过滤器，我推荐 winix，它经过了项目农场的测试，得分最高。我也是两个孩子的主人，他们已经工作了 4 年，现在我使用自动运行它们，再也不用担心它们了，自动睡眠模式真是太幸福了。

Aliexpress 上的替换品很便宜，价格约为 30 美元（每年）。您不需要真正的高效过滤器替代品，如果您没有异味问题或通风良好，则可以跳过碳过滤器。

每次我更换过滤器时，过滤器都是漆黑的，所以它们肯定在做一些事情。

不过，我确实建议跳过所有这些，只购买一个带有较低层 Merv 过滤器的箱式风扇，因为归根结底，气流最重要，如果断电，它会自行重新打开，而且它还让您能够将其与家庭自动化相结合。

If anyone is looking into getting an air filter I recommend winix it was tested by project farm and scored the highest. also I am the owner of two and they've been working great for 4 years now I run them using auto and never worry about them again, the automatic sleep mode is bliss.

replacements are cheap on aliexpress coming at around $30 (per year). You don't need true hepa replacements, you can skip carbon filters if you don't have odor problems or have lots of ventilation.

the filters are pitch black every time I replace them so they're definitely doing something.

I do however recommend skipping all of that and just getting a box fan with a lower-tier merv filter since at the end of the day airflow matters the most and it turns itself back on if the power goes out plus it gives you the ability to tie it into home automation.

bertil 2026/04/11 22:17
公关的经典案例利用对一个结果的真实、轶事观察，但完全翻转它以假装这是一个系统结果，以对有关微塑料的所有科学发现提出质疑。最后一个故事背后的同一家公司也做了同样的事情来减缓监管，以限制吸烟、酒精、加工食品、炼油厂、全球变暖、铅管……的影响。
Classic case of PR leveraging a real, anecdotic observation on one single result, but completely flipping it to pretend it’s a systematic result, to saw doubt on all scientific findings around microplastic. The same companies behind this last story have done the same thing to slow down regulation to limit the impact of smoking, alcohol, processed foods, oil refinery, global warming, lead pipes…

bertil 2026/04/12 00:17
我希望数以百万计的因安全立法延迟数十年而丧生的人知道，假装开玩笑（后来被称为“随机混蛋”方法）也是那些公关公司惯用的策略，目的是让批评者听起来像个酸溜溜的人。
I wish the millions of people killed by delaying safety legislation for decades knew that pretending to make jokes (what became known as the "stochastic asshole" approach) was also a common tactic taught by those PR firms, to make critics sound like sour-puss.

在 HN 中查看
bertil 2026/04/12 02:11
你知道苏联操纵情报技术中的“有用的白痴”是什么吗？

一个人无论是开玩笑还是无意地重复一个被放在某个地方可以否认的论点。一个实验室在研究了一项小型研究后发表了一项修正，称他们的估计是错误的，因为他们没有意识到他们的手套是如何解释这一点的。你知道谁知道这件事吗？每个实验室的每个实习生。这是一个微小的更正，除了原始报告的 10 名读者之外，任何人都不应该看到。

但奇怪的是，这个故事得到了媒体的广泛报道：通常的“科学”出版物、商业媒体，甚至是大众媒体。为什么？因为它被呈现为“他们正在编造有关微塑料的东西”的作品，而这些作品可以走得很远。这种报道并非偶然发生。

所以不，我不认为你是故意这样做的。但我知道你最近读到过它；我知道你没有检查触发报道的原始故事是什么；我知道你觉得这很奇怪——而且我没有理由认为你故意试图传播错误信息。但是，你做到了。因为想要散布怀疑的人知道他们在做什么。

Do you know what a "useful idiot" is, in the Soviet manipulation tradecraft?

Someone who repeats, jokingly or not, an argument that was placed somewhere deniable. One lab, looking at a small study, published a correction saying their estimates were wrong because they didn’t realize how their gloves accounted for it. Do you know who knew about that? Every intern in every lab ever. This was a minor correction that should never has reached anyone except the 10 readers of their original report.

But, strangely, that story got a wide coverage in the press: the usual “science” publication, the trade press, even widespread media. Why? Because it was presented as a “They are making things up about micro-plastics” piece, and those can go really far. And that kind of coverage doesn’t happen by accident.

So no, I don’t think you did that deliberately. But I know you read about it recently; I know you didn’t check what that original story was that triggered the coverage; I know you found that quaint—and I have no reason to think you deliberately tried to spread misinformation. But, you did. Because the people who want to sow doubt know what they are doing.
